flashcards.back: A software component providing APIs for thread creation, synchronization, and management, abstracting OS complexities.
Scheduled in user space; kernel unaware of thread states. Advantage: Portability. Disadvantage: Blocking calls stall entire process.
Direct OS management allows non-blocking I/O and efficient scheduling, improving responsiveness.
Combines ULT and KLT, where multiple user-level threads map to fewer kernel-level threads for balanced performance.
Enable concurrency, handle synchronization (e.g., mutexes, semaphores), and ensure thread-safe resource access.
